claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period; Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you a few questions. Child Benefit is assessed on a per-child basis, so I'll need to understand your situation for each child you're inquiring about.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have a child (or children) you're responsible for? If yes, how old is the child (or how old are the children)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I can claim Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 18 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 18 years old.

Since Alex is 18 (which falls in the 16-19 age range), his eligibility for Child Benefit depends on whether he's in approved education or training.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** 

This would typically include things like:
- A-levels or similar qualifications
- T-levels
- Scottish Highers
- NVQs up to level 3
- Home education (if it started before they turned 16)

Or has he recently left education (within the last 20 weeks)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in education anymore. He left school a while back - more than 20 weeks ago now. He's been working full-time at a local warehouse to earn some money.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 18, left education more than 20 weeks ago, and is now working full-time, I need to confirm a few details to make my final assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Just to confirm before I give you my final assessment:

**Are you responsible for Alex - meaning does he live with you, or do you contribute at least the same as Child Benefit would pay towards his costs?**

I need to verify this to complete the eligibility assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home. He's still living here while he's working.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. I now have all the information I need to make a final assessment of your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the information you've provided:

- **Alex is 18 years old**
- **He is not currently in approved education or training**
- **He left education more than 20 weeks ago**
- **He is working full-time**
- **He lives with you**

**Unfortunately, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why: Child Benefit is only available for:
- Children under 16, OR
- Young people aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training (at least 12 hours per week of supervised study)

Since Alex is 18 and no longer in approved education or training, he does not qualify as a "qualifying child" for Child Benefit purposes. Additionally, when a 16 or 17-year-old leaves education, Child Benefit can continue for up to 20 weeks, but since Alex left more than 20 weeks ago and is now 18, this extension period has passed.

The fact that Alex lives with you and is working full-time doesn't change this - the key factor is that he's over 16 and not in approved education or training.

I'm sorry this isn't the outcome you were hoping for. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Thank you for explaining that to me. It's disappointing, but I understand now - since Alex isn't in education or training anymore and he's working full-time, he doesn't count as a qualifying child. I appreciate you taking the time to go through it all with me.
